name: Influence of Surface Temperature on Albedo and Greenhouse Effect

behavior.value:
[Surface_Energy] / ( {4180 Joules / kg / Degrees Celsius} * {1000 kg / cubic meter} * [Mixing_Height] * pi * [Radius_of_planet]^2) - {273 Degrees Celsius}

# Energy = specific heat * mass * temp

{3.86e26 Watts} * ([Radius_of_planet] / [Distance_from_sun])^2 / 4

# The sun's total radiation is 3.86Ã10^26 Watts.  From https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Solar_constant#The_Sun.27s_total_radiation
# Incoming solar radiation = total radiation * (Shadow area of planet) / (Surface area of sphere at planet distance)
# At Earth's distance, the incoming radiation density should be {1367 Watts / square meter}.

{5.67e-8 Watts / square meter / Degrees Celsius^4} * 4 * pi * [Radius_of_planet]^2 * ({273 Degrees Celsius} + [Surface_Temperature])^4

## blackbody emission, Stefan-Boltzmann Law
## whole surface (4 pi r^2) emits

{5.67e-8 Watts / square meter / Degrees Celsius^4} * 4 * pi * [Radius_of_planet]^2 * ({273 Degrees Celsius} + [Atmosphere_Temp])^4

## blackbody emission, Stefan-Boltzmann Law
## whole surface (4 pi r^2) emits

(1 - [Greenhouse_Effect]) * [Emitted_Surface_Radiation] + {5.67e-8 Watts / square meter / Degrees Celsius^4} * 4 * pi * [Radius_of_planet]^2 * ({273 Degrees Celsius} + [Atmosphere_Temp])^4

# First term represents radiation that passes through atmosphere.
# Second term represents blackbody emission, Stefan-Boltzmann Law
# whole surface (4 pi r^2) emits

[Atmosphere_Energy] / ( {1004 Joules / kilograms / Degrees Celsius} * [Mass_of_atmosphere]) - {273 Degrees Celsius}

# Energy = specific heat * mass * temp
# specific heat = 1004 J/kg/C from Hartmann
# "The atmosphere has a mass of about 5.15Ã10^18 kg" - https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Atmosphere_of_Earth
# It's convenient to have the temp in Celsius so subtract off 273C.

1-[Surface_Albedo]*0.05


#Surface albedo is a function dependent on temperature, thus making Greenhouse Effect also a function of temperature
behavior.description: The fraction of surface radiation absorbed by the atmosphereâ.  The absorbed radiation heats up the atmosphere causing it to radiate out (back down to the planet and out into space).  Surface radiation that isn't absorbed continues out to space.

The fraction of incoming solar radiation that is reflected back out to space.  Radiation that isn't reflected is absorbed by the surface.

"The average albedo of  Earth  is about 0.3." -- from  Wikipedia . However, when it snows, the albedo reaches as high as 0.9. I assume in this model that below 0ËC indicates snowing, and now the albedo is 0.9.

"The primary heat source for the ocean is solar radiation entering through the top surface.  Almost all of the solar energy flux into the ocean is aboxrbed in the top 100 m." -- Dennis L. Hartmann,  Global Physical Climatology  (1994).
"The  average distance   between the Earth and the Sun is   149.60 million kilometers " --  Wikipedia
